clues: A medication in this class was initially used by a French army surgeon around 1950 in a 'lytic cocktail' to treat surgical shock
The introduction of these drugs led to a massive deinstitutionalization of patients
First generation options were derived from methylene blue but caused significant sedation and involuntary movements leading to development of second generation options
Ancient physicians including Hippocrates described hearing 'the boiling of vinegar' as an early findings of this disease
Historically known as dropsy, this disease was commonly treated with bloodletting until William Withering began to use foxglove in 1785
Primarily divided into two types, this disease is the number one cause of hospitalizations for Americans on Medicare
Isolated in 1804, this drug was named after an ancient Greek deity known as the god of dreams
In 1898, Bayer introduced heroin as a safer cough suppressant and alternative to this medication, although this was later found to be more addictive leading to major backlash
This medication is a purified, standardized form of humanity's oldest analgesic derived from the Papaver somniferum plant and revolutionized pain control during the American Civil War
While rats are the main carriers of this disease, it also causes major outbreaks in California sea lions
A widespread zoonotic disease, this is also known as 'rice field jaundice' and 'mud-fever'
Severe form of this disease is also known as Weil's disease involving jaundice, renal failure, and hemorrhage
So deadly at one point, it was nicknamed the 'blue death' because it caused skin to turn blue-black as patients suffocated
The common name for this comes from 17th century Italy where outbreaks were believed to be under the influence of the stars or planets
Most vaccines for this are still made using fertilized chicken eggs, but the amount of egg protein in a typical dose is extremely low and not enough to trigger most allergic reactions
